workflows-reference.purpose: Provide workflows for preserving and extending an existing project architecture.

documentation-context.rule: Inspect existing code, docs, tests, package scripts, and CI before choosing an implementation shape.
When docs are still draft, fill them from observed evidence before broad implementation work.

task-memory-gate.rule: For long or risky work with at least two concrete implementation slices after alignment or design, load Compass references/task-memory.xml.
Implementation may not start until the task memory outcome is reported as created, resumed, or not-required.
When task memory is required, docs/.tasks/<task>/goal.md, diagram.md, and memories.md must exist before the first implementation edit or command.
Missing docs/_templates task memory files are a documentation gap, not permission to skip this gate.

workflow.description: Add one capability by observing, preserving, and extending the existing architecture.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: evidence discovery, fit design, implementation.
Do not introduce competing architecture names or folders without a decision record.
Implementation must use the existing composition, testing, naming, and dependency style unless a change is approved.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long or risky multi-slice feature.

phase.name: evidence-discovery
phase.purpose: Discover how the current project already organizes similar behavior.

actions.action: Read existing lock docs and inspect similar modules, tests, commands, and composition points.
Summarize the current architecture, principles, workflow, and commands from evidence.
Identify the smallest existing place where the new behavior belongs.
Record ambiguity before designing.

phase-output.existing-evidence: Files, docs, commands, and conventions observed.
phase-output.placement: Likely module, package, or component to extend.

phase.name: fit-design
phase.purpose: Design the feature to fit the current architecture instead of replacing it.

actions.action: Define behavior and tests in the project's existing style.
State how the design follows existing boundaries and naming.
List any architecture tension and whether it requires a decision record.
Define verification using existing scripts or commands.

phase-output.docs-impact: Existing lock docs to fill or update.

phase.name: implementation
phase.purpose: Implement one approved slice while preserving existing structure.

actions.action: Use existing patterns and commands.
Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Do not create new root architecture folders without approval.
Run the closest existing verification.
Stop after each verified slice.

phase-output.architecture-fit: How the change fits existing structure.

workflow.evidence: Observed architecture evidence, approved fit design, existing-style tests, and updated lock docs when needed.

workflow.description: Change existing behavior by controlling the delta from known baseline behavior.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: baseline-discovery, delta-design, implementation-checkpoints, and rollout-cleanup when rollout guards or compatibility paths are introduced.
Classify as architecture_change first when the update changes boundaries, dependency direction, module structure, platform categories, or cross-module contracts.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long, risky, or multi-slice update.
Implement one approved delta slice at a time and stop after verification.
High-risk updates must define rollback, feature-flag, dark-launch, compatibility, or cleanup evidence before implementation.

phase.name: baseline-discovery
phase.purpose: Protect current behavior before deciding what may change.

actions.action: Find the existing owner, similar behavior, tests, docs, commands, and composition points.
State the current behavior, public contracts, integrations, configuration, and user-visible expectations that must remain stable.
Classify update risk as minor, medium, or high based on blast radius, reversibility, data impact, user visibility, and boundary impact.
Record ambiguity before designing the delta.

phase-output.baseline: Existing owner, behavior, tests, docs, and verification commands observed.
phase-output.preserve-list: Behavior, contracts, and non-goals that must not drift.
phase-output.risk-level: minor, medium, or high with reason.

phase.name: delta-design
phase.purpose: Define the exact behavior change and the regression evidence before editing.

actions.action: State old behavior, new behavior, unchanged behavior, and intentionally removed behavior if any.
Define regression tests for preserved behavior and changed-behavior tests in the existing project style.
Define the smallest owning module or component to change.
Decide whether the update needs a feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, rollback plan, monitoring, docs update, or decision record.
Define the implementation slices and verification command for each slice.

phase-output.delta: Approved behavior change and unchanged behavior.
phase-output.rollout-risk: Flag, rollback, monitoring, compatibility, cleanup, or not needed.
phase-output.docs-impact: Docs or decisions to update, or none.

phase.name: implementation-checkpoints
phase.purpose: Apply one approved delta slice while keeping the baseline protected.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Add or update the approved regression and changed-behavior tests for the current slice when practical.
Change the smallest existing module or component and avoid introducing competing patterns.
Run the current slice verification command.
Stop after each verified slice and ask before continuing.

phase.name: rollout-cleanup
phase.purpose: Close temporary risk controls introduced for the update.

actions.action: Record feature flag, dark launch, compatibility path, monitoring, rollback, or cleanup status when any were introduced.
Remove stale flags or inactive compatibility paths after the update is fully accepted.
Update docs, decisions, public contracts, or runbooks when behavior or rollout expectations changed.
Run final verification for the touched scope.

phase-output.rollout-state: Flag, rollout, compatibility, rollback, or cleanup state.

workflow.evidence: Approved baseline, approved delta design, regression coverage for preserved behavior, changed-behavior verification, slice checkpoints, and rollout or cleanup evidence when needed.

steps.step: State why existing architecture can no longer absorb the change.
Compare preserving, adapting, and replacing the existing pattern.
Implement migration in small compatibility-preserving slices.
Verify existing behavior and new boundary behavior.

workflow.evidence: Decision record, compatibility evidence, and verification.
